title: Lindauer will stay on ballot
summary: Lindauer will stay on ballot By STEVE RINEHART Daily News reporter John Lindauer remained the Republican Party's candidate for governor Friday after a judge turned down an attempt to have him taken off the Nov. 3 ballot. Anchorage Superior Court Judge Brian Shortell issued the ruling in a civil lawsuit filed by a Chugiak woman who alleged that Lindauer broke state campaign finance laws. Shortell said evidence presented in the suit, against the state Division of Elections, was not convincing enough for him to stop state officials from certifying Lindauer's primary election victory. He said that neither the state nor Lindauer had had enough time to respond to her contentions and that the state's attorneys had made "a convincing argument that such an order would severely disrupt the elections…
